conceptClusters.title: Retrieved rule loading attribute and state evidence
exitEvidence: Learner retrieves valid declaration boundaries, complete stylesheet loading evidence, exact attribute match sets, and activated pseudo-class states before resolving conflicts.
Learner separates whether a declaration exists and matches from whether it wins, computes, remains usable, and survives changed source or state.

conceptClusters.title: Value processing defaulting inheritance and global keywords
exitEvidence: Learner distinguishes declared, cascaded, specified, computed, used, and actual values and identifies when defaulting selects inheritance or an initial value.
Learner predicts initial, inherit, unset, revert, revert-layer, and bounded all-property consequences without erasing required semantics or user-agent behavior.

conceptClusters.title: Complete cascade provenance and specificity columns
exitEvidence: Learner traces every candidate through relevance, origin and importance, encapsulation context, style attributes, layers, specificity, scope proximity, and order before naming a winner.
Learner compares A-B-C specificity columns and the exact :is(), :not(), :has(), :where(), and child-index argument rules without decimal arithmetic or escalation.

conceptClusters.title: Layer ordering bounded scope and maintainable overrides
exitEvidence: Learner declares stable layer order, distinguishes normal from important reversal, accounts for unlayered rules, and integrates external styles without editing their source.

Learner uses @scope only as explicitly work-in-progress behavior, predicts scope boundaries and proximity in named supported environments, and retains a simpler verified architecture when it is unnecessary.

conceptClusters.title: Link state privacy input parity and user preference resilience
exitEvidence: Learner traces link, visited, hover, focus-visible, and active conflicts through the full cascade instead of memorizing one declaration-order slogan.
Learner preserves visible unobscured focus, pointer and keyboard parity, non-color state cues, forced-color usability, and visited-history privacy across changed environments.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A compact evidence board grows from matched declarations into value provenance, complete cascade traces, specificity columns, layer and scope order, and resilient link states.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner can load a stylesheet and predict selector and pseudo-class matches but has not separated matching from winning or computed value provenance.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner can narrate one complete changed conflict from source and state through the winning declaration, computed result, rendered behavior, and accessibility limits.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names the first incorrect provenance stage, shows only the observed candidate evidence at that stage, and requests a new prediction before revealing later stages.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Worked guidance reveals one comparison dimension at a time while keeping the final winner, value, and changed-environment result for the learner to determine.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A failed model prediction routes to a two-declaration parallel case at the same failing dimension, then returns to the original full trace after an explained correction.

interactions.title: Recover declaration candidates
interactions.learnerAction: Label selector, declaration, property, value, importance annotation, and source position before collecting only declarations that can affect one target property.
interactions.artifactChange: A source panel gains rule-anatomy labels and a candidate-declaration ledger.

evidence.observable: Ledger contains every syntactically valid matching candidate and excludes malformed, unmatched, and wrong-property declarations.
evidence.changedCase: Insert one malformed declaration, one near-match attribute selector, and one valid candidate later in source.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Candidate collection before any cascade comparison begins.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reparse the smallest rule, recompute its exact match set, then rebuild only the affected ledger rows.

interactions.title: Prove every stylesheet source
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect resolved URL, response identity, media applicability, parsed rule, inline source, and source order before treating an apparent declaration as cascade input.
interactions.artifactChange: Candidate ledger gains stylesheet identity, applicability, and source-position columns.

evidence.observable: Each candidate traces to an applicable loaded stylesheet or style attribute rather than a filename assumption.
evidence.changedCase: Change one link path, one media condition, and one style block position while retaining similar filenames.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Loaded and applicable source evidence for each candidate declaration.
interactions.correctionRoute: Follow the first missing network or parse link, restore exact source identity, then recollect candidates.

interactions.title: Separate match from state
interactions.learnerAction: Predict which attribute and pseudo-class candidates match in idle, keyboard-focus, pointer-hover, activation, and changed-attribute states without naming a winner yet.
interactions.artifactChange: Candidate ledger gains per-state match booleans and rejected-candidate reasons.

evidence.observable: Complete matched sets change only when the actual attribute or interaction state changes and remain independent of cascade priority.
evidence.changedCase: Mutate a token attribute, move focus by keyboard, hover a descendant, and activate the link separately.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Exact per-state matched sets before cascade filtering and sorting.
interactions.correctionRoute: Activate one state at a time, inspect the exact subject, and redraw the set without comparing declarations.

interactions.title: Trace property value stages
interactions.learnerAction: Follow one property from zero or many declared values through cascaded, specified, computed, used, and actual stages while identifying the stage that answers each inspection question.
interactions.artifactChange: A value-provenance pipeline records one result and evidence surface per processing stage.

evidence.observable: Pipeline distinguishes no cascaded value, defaulting, computed dependency resolution, layout use, and environment-constrained actual output.
evidence.changedCase: Remove the only declaration, change the inherited parent value, and constrain the display environment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First value-processing stage whose input or result is confused.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to that stage's definition, compare only its input and output, then resume the downstream trace.

interactions.title: Predict explicit defaulting
interactions.learnerAction: Predict inherited and non-inherited results for initial, inherit, unset, revert, and revert-layer without treating the keywords as interchangeable reset commands.
interactions.artifactChange: Value pipeline gains keyword-specific upstream source and resulting specified-value rows.

evidence.observable: Each keyword selects its defined initial, parent, origin, or layer source for both inherited and non-inherited properties.
evidence.changedCase: Swap color for margin, add a user-agent default, add an earlier author layer, and change the parent value.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Keyword semantics and property inheritance classification.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify the property first, then trace the named keyword to its defined upstream value source.

interactions.title: Explain complete cascade sorting
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why the winning declaration survives each cascade dimension in order instead of jumping from selector appearance or source position directly to the result.
interactions.artifactChange: Cascade ladder records candidates removed at relevance, origin and importance, context, style attribute, layer, specificity, scope, and order.

evidence.observable: One surviving declaration has a complete provenance chain and every losing candidate has a first decisive comparison.
evidence.changedCase: Toggle a media condition, add user-important input, move an author rule, and start a transition independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First skipped or misordered cascade comparison dimension.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore all candidates at the last correct rung, apply only the next comparison, and explain each removal.

interactions.title: Compare specificity as columns
interactions.learnerAction: Count A-B-C columns for compound selectors and predict :is(), :not(), :has(), :where(), and child-index argument contributions without decimal conversion.
interactions.artifactChange: Specificity table gains per-selector columns, functional-argument rule, matching subject, and comparison result.

evidence.observable: Columns use the selector that actually matches and preserve lexicographic comparison across ID, class-like, and type-like counts.
evidence.changedCase: Change the matching :is() branch, replace it with :where(), add an attribute, and remove an ID.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Exact selector component or functional rule producing the first wrong column.
interactions.correctionRoute: Mark selector components by category, apply the function rule, then compare columns left to right.

interactions.title: Inspect normal and important layers
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect declared layer order, nested and anonymous identity, unlayered author rules, normal priority, and important-priority reversal before comparing selector specificity.
interactions.artifactChange: Layer map displays first declaration order, nesting, implicit layers, importance direction, and winning layer.

evidence.observable: Normal and important declarations produce the specified opposite layer direction and unlayered placement without specificity leakage across layers.
evidence.changedCase: Reorder only the layer statement, add an unlayered rule, make both declarations important, and nest one layer.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Layer identity, first declaration order, implicit placement, or importance direction.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild the global layer order before inspecting declarations inside the first surviving layer.

interactions.title: Bound experimental scope reasoning
interactions.learnerAction: Read a supported @scope case as work-in-progress, identify start and optional end boundaries, then place scope proximity after specificity and before order without adding prelude specificity.
interactions.artifactChange: Scope map records supported environment, roots, limits, subjects, proximity hops, specificity, and order.

evidence.observable: Only in-scope subjects compete, equal-specificity candidates use defined proximity, and unsupported or unnecessary cases keep simpler verified selectors.
evidence.changedCase: Nest a second component, add a scope limit, move the subject closer, and test one named environment without scope.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Scope support boundary, subject inclusion, proximity, or specificity placement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Check environment support, draw boundaries, filter subjects, then compare specificity, proximity, and order.

interactions.title: Explain resilient link states
interactions.learnerAction: Explain complete link-state behavior across unvisited, visited, hover, focus-visible, active, forced-color, and obscured-focus cases using full cascade traces and privacy limits.
interactions.artifactChange: Link matrix records input path, actual state, winning declarations, non-color cues, focus visibility, obstruction, and privacy-safe output.

evidence.observable: Every input and preference case retains link identity and visible focus without exposing history-dependent content or computed differences.
evidence.changedCase: Change declaration order, visit one partitioned URL, enable forced colors, remove hover capability, and open an obscuring header.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First state, cascade, accessibility, environment, or privacy invariant that fails.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reproduce one input and environment case, trace its winner, restore a redundant cue, then rerun the matrix.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: Learner incrementally repairs and architects a public-records request portal whose document remains semantic while its stylesheet gains explainable values, conflicts, layers, bounded scope, and resilient links.
activityDesigns.startingState: Portal HTML and one loaded stylesheet contain intentional attribute hooks and states, plus inherited, origin, specificity, layer, scope, and link conflicts that have not been traced.
activityDesigns.completionState: Portal passes complete winner traces and changed state, source, layer, scope, keyboard, pointer, forced-color, privacy, and obstruction tests without specificity escalation.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback reports observed candidate and first divergent cascade dimension, connects it to the named competency, and asks for the smallest trace or source correction.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Three progressive hints identify the comparison model, then the relevant evidence panel, then bounded syntax while withholding the winning declaration and final source edit.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Failure opens a smaller public-notice parallel conflict, requires a fresh prediction and explanation, then returns to the portal with changed selectors and declaration order.

interactions.title: Collect valid candidate rules
interactions.learnerAction: Predict which portal declarations are syntactically valid, target the status link, set the requested property, and therefore enter the candidate ledger.
interactions.artifactChange: Status-link candidate ledger receives valid and rejected source rows.

evidence.observable: Ledger includes every valid matching property declaration and a cause for every excluded source row.
evidence.changedCase: Insert a malformed value, a different property, and a valid later rule with similar punctuation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Syntax, property identity, and exact match requirements for candidate entry.
interactions.correctionRoute: Annotate one rejected rule token by token, repair only its first invalid boundary, and recollect candidates.

interactions.title: Verify portal style sources
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect the public portal's base, agency, print, and inline style sources for exact response identity, applicability, parsed rules, and document order.
interactions.artifactChange: Source provenance panel records URL, response, media, parser, and order evidence.

evidence.observable: Every cascade candidate points to an actually loaded and applicable source with a stable document-order position.
evidence.changedCase: Break the agency URL, switch print emulation, move the internal style block, and preserve a misleading filename.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First unproven link between source declaration and active cascade input.
interactions.correctionRoute: Follow network, applicability, parsing, and order in sequence, then update only affected candidates.

interactions.title: Predict request-status matches
interactions.learnerAction: Predict complete matches for presence, exact, and token attribute selectors across open, delayed, closed, and near-match public-request records.
interactions.artifactChange: Candidate rows gain expected matched record identities for each attribute contract.

evidence.observable: Each selector accepts only record values allowed by its operator and semantic status contract.
evidence.changedCase: Add status-open, uppercase OPEN, a multi-token value, an empty value, and a DOM-updated record.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Operator semantics and complete accepted versus rejected record sets.
interactions.correctionRoute: List value tokens literally, apply one operator definition, and compare the result with status meaning.

interactions.title: Activate genuine link states
interactions.learnerAction: Test idle, keyboard focus, pointer hover, activation, target, and visited states separately while recording actual matches before any winner comparison.
interactions.artifactChange: State matrix records activation path and matching candidate IDs for every portal link state.

evidence.observable: Candidates appear only while their real state exists and keyboard and pointer paths remain independently observable.
evidence.changedCase: Move focus by Tab, hover a child icon, press and hold activation, change the fragment, and revisit a local URL.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Actual state activation and exact selector subject before cascade sorting.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove forced state classes, reproduce one genuine input transition, and inspect the matching subject.

interactions.title: Explain portal value provenance
interactions.learnerAction: Explain declared, cascaded, specified, computed, used, and actual values for the request-link color and identify which evidence surface answers each stage.
interactions.artifactChange: Portal value inspector gains stage-specific values, sources, and inspection evidence.

evidence.observable: Trace distinguishes cascade output, defaulting, dependency resolution, layout use, and environment-constrained rendering.
evidence.changedCase: Remove all child declarations, change the parent color, enable forced colors, and disconnect a copied node.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First value stage incorrectly merged with another processing stage.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare only input and output at the first divergent stage, then rebuild downstream values.

interactions.title: Predict inherited status text
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the request-card descendants' specified and computed color when the card value changes and descendants have zero, inherited, or explicit declarations.
interactions.artifactChange: Value tree records parent and descendant color sources plus explicit override boundaries.

evidence.observable: Descendants with no cascaded color inherit the parent computed value while explicit declarations retain their own cascade result.
evidence.changedCase: Add a nested emphasis element, move it under another parent, and add then remove a local color rule.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Property inheritance behavior and nearest computed parent source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove selector comparisons, locate the nearest parent computed value, then restore explicit child candidates.

interactions.title: Predict non-inherited spacing
interactions.learnerAction: Predict descendant margin values when only the request card has margin, then distinguish initial defaulting from inheritance in the resulting boxes.
interactions.artifactChange: Value tree adds non-inherited margin rows and inspected box evidence.

evidence.observable: Descendants without cascaded margin receive the property's initial value rather than the parent's computed margin.
evidence.changedCase: Add two nested descendants, set parent margin to a new value, and explicitly inherit on only one child.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Confusion between no declaration, initial value, and inherited value.
interactions.correctionRoute: Check the property's inheritance definition, then trace zero-candidate defaulting on each element.

interactions.title: Use initial deliberately
interactions.learnerAction: Set one inherited request-card property to initial, predict the specification-defined initial result, and verify that it does not mean browser default appearance.
interactions.artifactChange: One bounded child rule uses initial and records its before and after provenance.

evidence.observable: Child specified value comes from the property's initial value and remains distinct from parent and user-agent declarations.
evidence.changedCase: Change the parent value and add a user-agent-like rule while keeping the explicit initial declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Exact initial-value source and its distinction from origin rollback.
interactions.correctionRoute: Look up the property's initial value, compare it with parent and UA candidates, then rerun the trace.

interactions.title: Force inheritance deliberately
interactions.learnerAction: Apply inherit to one normally non-inherited border color, then prove the child receives the parent's computed value rather than copying source text.
interactions.artifactChange: Child border rule gains explicit inheritance and parent-to-child computed-value evidence.

evidence.observable: Child computed border color follows the parent computed value across source representations and later parent changes.
evidence.changedCase: Change the parent from a named color to a system color and introduce a higher-priority parent declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Inheritance of computed value rather than declaration token copying.
interactions.correctionRoute: Resolve the parent cascade and computed value first, then use that result as the child's inherited input.

interactions.title: Separate unset outcomes
interactions.learnerAction: Use unset on one inherited and one non-inherited property, predict its inherit-like and initial-like branches, and verify both after a parent change.
interactions.artifactChange: Two portal declarations use unset with property-classification evidence.

evidence.observable: Unset selects inheritance for the inherited property and initial value for the non-inherited property.
evidence.changedCase: Swap color and padding values, change the parent, and remove all lower-origin child declarations.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Property classification that controls the unset branch.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify both properties before evaluating unset, then recalculate their specified values separately.

interactions.title: Test origin and layer rollback
interactions.learnerAction: Test revert, revert-layer, and a bounded all declaration against user-agent, user, author-layer, unlayered, and custom-property inputs without erasing document direction.
interactions.artifactChange: Rollback matrix records each keyword's excluded and newly visible value source.

evidence.observable: Revert crosses an origin boundary, revert-layer crosses the current layer boundary, and all respects its defined exclusions.
evidence.changedCase: Add an earlier layer, a user declaration, a custom property, direction metadata, and an unlayered author rule.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Exact origin, layer, property, or all-property rollback boundary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Draw source groups by origin and layer, remove only the keyword-defined group, then resume the cascade.

interactions.title: Filter irrelevant declarations
interactions.learnerAction: Predict which screen, print, support-query, and nonmatching-state declarations remain relevant to the public-record link before comparing their priority.
interactions.artifactChange: Cascade ladder gains a relevance rung with environment and state reasons.

evidence.observable: Only declarations applicable to the target property, element, state, and current condition survive relevance filtering.
evidence.changedCase: Switch print mode, change a supports result, focus the link, and remove an attribute token independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First environment, selector, state, or property condition evaluated incorrectly.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate each condition independently, intersect surviving candidates, then stop before priority sorting.

interactions.title: Inspect normal origin order
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect user-agent, user, and author normal declarations for a request link and identify the winning origin before looking at specificity or source order.
interactions.artifactChange: Candidate rows receive explicit origin and normal-importance classification.

evidence.observable: Author normal declarations outrank user and user-agent normal declarations only after all candidates are proven relevant.
evidence.changedCase: Add a more specific user-normal selector and move the user stylesheet after the author stylesheet.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Origin classification before selector specificity or source position.
interactions.correctionRoute: Hide selector text, compare origin and importance labels only, then restore later dimensions.

interactions.title: Preserve important user choices
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the winner when author, user, and user-agent declarations become important, then explain why author specificity cannot defeat a higher important origin.
interactions.artifactChange: Cascade ladder displays important-origin order and user-preference preservation.

evidence.observable: Important origin order is applied before layer and specificity, preserving the higher-priority user or UA declaration.
evidence.changedCase: Toggle importance on each origin and add an author selector containing two IDs.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Importance and origin comparison before specificity calculation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Group candidates by importance, order origins within the surviving group, then discard lower groups.

interactions.title: Place animation and transition values
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect an animated then transitioning status link and place animation and transition values correctly among normal and important cascade origins.
interactions.artifactChange: Cascade ladder gains time-bound animation and transition candidate bands.

evidence.observable: Active transition and animation values occupy their defined priority positions and disappear when their timelines end.
evidence.changedCase: Start and finish a transition, disable the animation, and add author-important and user-important declarations.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Time-bound candidate existence and its precise origin-order position.
interactions.correctionRoute: Freeze one timeline phase, list active candidates, compare origins, then repeat at the changed phase.

interactions.title: Place style attributes correctly
interactions.learnerAction: Predict how a relevant style attribute compares with style-rule declarations of the same origin and importance without representing it as an imaginary fourth specificity column.
interactions.artifactChange: Candidate ledger marks style-attribute context separately from selector specificity.

evidence.observable: Style attribute is compared at its defined cascade position and not encoded as a fabricated A-B-C-D score.
evidence.changedCase: Move an equivalent declaration into a style rule, toggle importance, and add a higher-priority origin.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Style-attribute cascade position versus selector specificity columns.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove the fake column, classify context and importance, then calculate specificity only for style rules.

interactions.title: Use order only as final tie break
interactions.learnerAction: Reorder two otherwise tied request-link rules, verify the changed winner, then add a higher-priority candidate that makes source movement irrelevant.
interactions.artifactChange: Two tied rules change order while the cascade ladder records when order becomes decisive.

evidence.observable: Order changes the winner only after all prior cascade dimensions remain tied.
evidence.changedCase: Reverse the tied rules, add an earlier higher layer, and add a later lower origin candidate.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Whether prior dimensions are genuinely tied before order is consulted.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare every earlier ladder rung, mark equality, then use document order only for surviving ties.

interactions.title: Test user preference resilience
interactions.learnerAction: Inject a user-important readability declaration and test that portal author layers, selectors, inline styles, and normal transitions do not silently defeat it.
interactions.artifactChange: Portal regression matrix gains a user-important preference case and winner trace.

evidence.observable: User-important value remains the winner unless a legitimately higher cascade origin is present.
evidence.changedCase: Add author-important, inline-important, animation, transition, and later-source candidates one at a time.

interactions.feedbackTarget: User-preference candidate retention across author architecture changes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Locate the first rung that wrongly removes user-important input and repair the classification, not selector strength.

interactions.title: Retest after source movement
interactions.learnerAction: Move a public-record rule between external and internal sources, reload, and rebuild its complete provenance rather than assuming equivalent source text means equivalent priority.
interactions.artifactChange: Source location changes and saved-reload evidence updates the candidate order trace.

evidence.observable: Winner trace reflects actual loaded source order and remains stable after save and reload.
evidence.changedCase: Move the rule across two linked sheets, an internal block, and an inline declaration while preserving value text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Actual document order and context after source relocation and reload.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reinspect loaded rules in document order, discard remembered positions, and regenerate candidate provenance.

interactions.title: Defend a complete winner trace
interactions.learnerAction: Explain the request-link winner and every loser's first decisive comparison across relevance, origin, importance, context, layer, specificity, scope, and order.
interactions.artifactChange: Portal stores one complete annotated conflict trace as a review checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Trace names all relevant candidates, one winner, and the earliest valid reason each other candidate loses.
evidence.changedCase: Change one condition at each cascade dimension and require a fresh winner explanation after every mutation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First unsupported claim or skipped dimension in the conflict defense.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore the last proven candidate set, evaluate one dimension, and rewrite only the affected defense sentence.

interactions.title: Count basic specificity columns
interactions.learnerAction: Count ID, class-like, and type-like columns for the portal's type, class, ID, attribute, pseudo-class, pseudo-element, and universal selectors.
interactions.artifactChange: Specificity table records component categories and A-B-C totals for each matching selector.

evidence.observable: Every selector component enters its defined column while combinators and universal selectors add no count.
evidence.changedCase: Replace a class with an attribute, add a pseudo-element, remove an ID, and insert extra combinators.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First selector component assigned to the wrong specificity column.
interactions.correctionRoute: Underline components, label each A, B, C, or ignored, then sum within columns only.

interactions.title: Compare columns lexicographically
interactions.learnerAction: Compare selectors left to right by A-B-C columns and prove that many type selectors never accumulate into one class or ID selector.
interactions.artifactChange: Comparator shows first decisive column rather than a decimal score.

evidence.observable: Comparison stops at the first unequal column and never carries counts between columns.
evidence.changedCase: Add twenty type selectors, then add one class and one ID to different competitors.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Decimal conversion, column carrying, or wrong first decisive column.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove punctuation between columns, compare A then B then C, and state the first inequality.

interactions.title: Trace matching is branches
interactions.learnerAction: Add :is() to group portal link hooks, record which argument matches each subject, and calculate the function's most-specific argument contribution.
interactions.artifactChange: Grouped selector and table gain :is() argument-match and specificity evidence.

evidence.observable: :is() contributes the most specific argument from its list under the defined matching process rather than one generic pseudo-class count.
evidence.changedCase: Add an ID branch, change which subjects match, remove that branch, and add a nonmatching high-specificity branch.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Argument-list specificity rule and matching-process context.
interactions.correctionRoute: List arguments and columns separately, mark the applicable specificity, then replace the function contribution.

interactions.title: Trace not argument weight
interactions.learnerAction: Use :not() to exclude archived records, then prove the function contributes its argument-list specificity without counting :not itself as an extra pseudo-class.
interactions.artifactChange: Archived-record selector and specificity explanation are added to the portal.

evidence.observable: :not() exclusion changes matches while its specificity is replaced by the most specific argument contribution.
evidence.changedCase: Swap a class exclusion for an ID and attribute list, then change the record's archive state.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Separation of matching exclusion from functional specificity replacement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compute the argument list independently, remove an extra pseudo-class count, then retest the match set.

interactions.title: Trace relational selector weight
interactions.learnerAction: Use :has() only to style a request card containing a delayed status link, then trace relational matching and argument specificity as separate evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Request-card relational rule gains match and specificity rows.

evidence.observable: Card match depends on the anchored relative selector while :has() specificity uses the defined argument-list contribution.
evidence.changedCase: Move the status link outside the card, change its token, and add a more specific nonmatching argument.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Anchored relationship matching versus specificity contribution.
interactions.correctionRoute: Prove the relative match first, calculate each argument column next, then evaluate the cascade.

interactions.title: Create zero-weight grouping
interactions.learnerAction: Replace a high-weight portal context selector with :where(), verify identical intended matches, and prove the grouping contributes zero specificity.
interactions.artifactChange: One context rule uses :where() and records lower conflict weight without broader matches.

evidence.observable: :where() arguments affect matching but contribute zero to A-B-C, leaving only selector components outside the function.
evidence.changedCase: Add ID and class arguments inside :where(), then add one class outside it and mutate the context.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Zero specificity replacement without loss of match-set precision.
interactions.correctionRoute: Circle components inside :where(), assign them zero weight, then count only remaining components.

interactions.title: Count child-index arguments
interactions.learnerAction: Predict specificity and matches for :nth-child() with an of selector list, including both the pseudo-class count and most-specific list argument.
interactions.artifactChange: Records list gains indexed-match and two-part specificity evidence.

evidence.observable: Specificity combines one pseudo-class with the defined selector-list contribution while matching uses filtered sibling position.
evidence.changedCase: Insert an unmatched sibling, change the of list, add an ID branch, and reverse record order.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Filtered position versus pseudo-class-plus-argument specificity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Build the filtered sibling list first, then add the pseudo-class B count to argument columns.

interactions.title: Repair without escalation
interactions.learnerAction: Explain and implement a portal override by correcting layer ownership or selector weight instead of appending an ID, deeper chain, duplicated class, or important annotation.
interactions.artifactChange: Escalating override is replaced with a bounded lower-weight rule and architecture note.

evidence.observable: Changed state still wins predictably while unrelated records remain unchanged and future selectors need no escalation.
evidence.changedCase: Add a new state class, nest the component, and reorder source while preserving declared layer ownership.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Cause-level conflict repair and stable selector weight after mutations.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify the first decisive cascade dimension, repair ownership there, and delete unnecessary selector weight.

interactions.title: Declare portal layer order
interactions.learnerAction: Declare reset, base, components, utilities, and exceptions order before their rules, then assign existing portal declarations without changing semantic HTML.
interactions.artifactChange: Portal stylesheet gains one explicit global layer statement and bounded layer blocks.

evidence.observable: First declarations establish stable layer identity and normal priority independent of block placement and selector weight.
evidence.changedCase: Move layer blocks, split one layer across sources, and add rules before and after the ordering statement.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First-declaration order, stable names, and correct rule ownership.
interactions.correctionRoute: List intended responsibilities and order first, declare them once, then reassign only misowned rules.

interactions.title: Predict unlayered normal rules
interactions.learnerAction: Predict how an unlayered normal author rule compares with every explicit normal layer before calculating either selector's specificity.
interactions.artifactChange: Layer map gains the implicit outer layer and an unlayered candidate.

evidence.observable: Relevant unlayered normal author declarations outrank declarations in explicit author layers regardless of cross-layer specificity.
evidence.changedCase: Add an unlayered type selector against a layered ID selector, then move the rule into a named layer.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Implicit unlayered placement before specificity comparison.
interactions.correctionRoute: Place every candidate on the layer map first and compare selector columns only within the winner layer.

interactions.title: Inspect nested layer identity
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect nested agency base and theme layers, their qualified names, implicit sublayers, and ordering without merging equal final name segments.
interactions.artifactChange: Layer map expands nested qualified names and implicit sublayer positions.

evidence.observable: Nested layers retain parent-qualified identity and rules outside nested blocks occupy the parent's implicit sublayer.
evidence.changedCase: Add a top-level base layer, repeat theme under another parent, and introduce one anonymous nested layer.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Qualified layer identity, nesting group, or implicit sublayer placement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Write full dotted identities, group by parent, then append each parent's implicit sublayer.

interactions.title: Test important layer reversal
interactions.learnerAction: Make competing portal layer declarations important and test the reversed layer direction while preserving higher user-important origin behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: Layer map gains separate normal and important arrows plus user-origin boundary.

evidence.observable: Earlier explicit author layers outrank later ones for important declarations without crossing a higher origin boundary.
evidence.changedCase: Toggle importance in first, middle, last, and unlayered positions and add user-important input.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Important reversal inside one origin and its separation from origin order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Choose origin and importance group first, reverse its layer map, then compare candidates inside the survivor.

interactions.title: Roll back one layer
interactions.learnerAction: Use revert-layer on one public-record exception, trace the newly exposed earlier layer or origin value, and keep unrelated properties unchanged.
interactions.artifactChange: Exception rule gains bounded revert-layer and property-specific provenance.

evidence.observable: Current layer's value is removed from consideration and the next eligible layer or origin supplies the property value.
evidence.changedCase: Add an earlier utility value, remove it, add an unlayered value, and compare normal with important rollback.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Exact layer removed and next eligible value source exposed.
interactions.correctionRoute: Delete candidates from only the current layer group, then resume the ordinary cascade with remaining rows.

interactions.title: Integrate external agency styles
interactions.learnerAction: Place an unchanged external agency stylesheet into a declared low-priority layer and override one portal need through local architecture instead of editing vendor source.
interactions.artifactChange: External source gains explicit layer ownership and one bounded local override.

evidence.observable: External declarations remain intact, local override wins by declared layer responsibility, and unrelated external behavior persists.
evidence.changedCase: Update external selector specificity, reorder its internal rules, and add a new component without changing local override weight.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source ownership and layer-level integration rather than vendor mutation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore external source exactly, assign it one layer, then place the local requirement in the intended higher layer.

interactions.title: Test one bounded scope
interactions.learnerAction: Apply @scope to repeated public-record widgets only in named supported environments, verify roots and limits, and compare a simpler class architecture in the unsupported case.
interactions.artifactChange: Scope map and environment matrix record roots, limits, subjects, proximity, and simpler architecture.

evidence.observable: Scoped rules affect only included widget subjects, proximity breaks eligible ties, and no unsupported behavior is claimed universal.
evidence.changedCase: Nest widgets, add a scope limit, move one subject closer, and run one environment with @scope unavailable.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Work-in-progress support claim, scope inclusion, limit, or proximity ordering.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name the environment, draw boundaries, filter subjects, then compare specificity, proximity, and order.

interactions.title: Collect link-state candidates
interactions.learnerAction: Predict complete candidates and winners for unvisited, visited, hover, focus-visible, and active request links without relying on one mnemonic rule order.
interactions.artifactChange: Link matrix gains state-specific candidate and full-winner columns.

evidence.observable: Each real state resolves through selector matching and complete cascade dimensions to a defensible visible result.
evidence.changedCase: Reorder declarations, change specificity, combine states, and add a user-important focus preference.

interactions.feedbackTarget: State match set and full cascade trace for each link condition.
interactions.correctionRoute: Activate one state, collect candidates, resolve full cascade, then repeat without reusing the prior winner.

interactions.title: Preserve pointer and keyboard cues
interactions.learnerAction: Style hover and focus-visible as distinct genuine states while preserving link affordance, a strong keyboard indicator, and equivalent task information for both paths.
interactions.artifactChange: Portal links gain bounded hover treatment and persistent focus-visible treatment with redundant cues.

evidence.observable: Pointer and keyboard users can locate and activate links, and focus remains visible without requiring hover capability.
evidence.changedCase: Disable hover capability, navigate only by keyboard, change the background, and add a competing active rule.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Input-path parity, visible focus, and state-specific cascade conflicts.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test keyboard first, restore a non-color focus cue, then add pointer treatment without overriding it.

interactions.title: Test simultaneous link states
interactions.learnerAction: Test focus-visible plus active and hover plus active combinations, ensuring the activation treatment never removes the persistent focus indicator.
interactions.artifactChange: Link matrix gains combined-state rows and retained-focus assertions.

evidence.observable: Combined states resolve predictably and the keyboard focus cue remains visible throughout activation and after release.
evidence.changedCase: Hold Space or Enter where applicable, press a pointer, reorder active declarations, and move focus after activation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Combined-state candidate set and focus persistence through activation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Freeze the combined state, trace all winners per property, and restore focus paint at the decisive cascade dimension.

interactions.title: Bound visited-state evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Explain a privacy-safe visited treatment that aids orientation without generated history labels, layout differences, prohibited property assumptions, or computed-style disclosure claims.
interactions.artifactChange: Visited-state design note records useful bounded styling and explicit privacy exclusions.

evidence.observable: Visited and unvisited paths remain usable while no task content, measurement, or diagnostic reveals private navigation history.
evidence.changedCase: Visit same- and cross-origin-like fixtures, query computed style, disable author styles, and linearize link content.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visited usefulness claim and privacy-safe evidence boundary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove history-dependent content and geometry, retain bounded orientation styling, and restate evidence limits.

interactions.title: Pass link preference matrix
interactions.learnerAction: Run keyboard, pointer, no-hover, changed-background, forced-color light and dark, zoom, and author-created obstruction cases against every portal link state.
interactions.artifactChange: Portal stores a complete link-state and preference regression matrix with causal traces.

evidence.observable: Links remain identifiable and operable, focus remains visible and at least partially unobscured, and no state depends on color alone.
evidence.changedCase: Enable each named environment, add a sticky obstruction, suppress shadows and backgrounds, and alter layer order.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First failing input, state, environment, focus, or distinguishability invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reproduce one matrix cell, trace its winning paint and geometry, repair cause, then rerun all cells.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: Learner completes a partially scaffolded school-family portal stylesheet and evidence notebook using new content, selectors, source layout, layer ownership, and link requirements.
activityDesigns.startingState: School portal provides semantic HTML, incomplete style sources, partial provenance columns, and no copied public-record rules or finished conflict decisions.
activityDesigns.completionState: School portal resolves inherited values, cascade conflicts, specificity, layer and scope decisions, and accessible link states with fewer prompts and complete changed-case evidence.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names the first unsupported provenance entry or failed behavior but leaves candidate collection, winning declaration, and repair choice for learner completion.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Faded hints identify a missing evidence category, then a relevant panel, then one constrained syntax location without supplying the complete trace or source solution.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Failure routes to a smaller attendance-message variant with changed selectors and values, then requires return and independent repair in the school portal.

interactions.title: Complete candidate anatomy
interactions.learnerAction: Complete missing candidate-ledger rows for valid, malformed, wrong-property, and unmatched school announcement declarations without comparing their priority.
interactions.artifactChange: Announcement ledger receives exact source anatomy and exclusion reasons.

evidence.observable: Every valid matching property declaration enters the ledger exactly once and every excluded row has a parser or match cause.
evidence.changedCase: Add a near-match data-audience value, malformed important annotation, and valid internal declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Candidate boundary before cascade priority reasoning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Annotate the first disputed source rule and recompute only its syntax, property, and match eligibility.

interactions.title: Prove school style inputs
interactions.learnerAction: Test family, district, print, and inline style sources for load, applicability, parse, order, and saved-reload evidence before trusting their candidate rows.
interactions.artifactChange: School source panel gains independently collected provenance and reload evidence.

evidence.observable: Candidate rows reference actual active sources and update after media, path, and document-order mutations.
evidence.changedCase: Break the district path, switch print mode, reorder internal styles, and reload after saving.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First missing source-to-candidate evidence link.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace network, media, parser, document order, and reload until the first divergence is located.

interactions.title: Complete audience match sets
interactions.learnerAction: Complete presence, exact, and token attribute match sets for student, family, staff, and near-match announcement audiences before resolving conflicts.
interactions.artifactChange: Announcement candidates receive complete subject identities for each audience selector.

evidence.observable: Every match set follows operator semantics and the school's explicit audience data contract.
evidence.changedCase: Add a multi-audience token, empty value, case change, suffix near-match, and DOM mutation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Accepted and rejected values for each attribute operator.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare literal values against one operator definition, then reconcile with the audience contract.

interactions.title: Test school link states
interactions.learnerAction: Activate actual hover, focus-visible, active, target, and visited states for schedule links and record candidates without permanent state classes.
interactions.artifactChange: Schedule state matrix receives input paths and exact matching candidates.

evidence.observable: State candidates appear only during genuine browser state and target the correct schedule link subjects.
evidence.changedCase: Tab between links, hover nested icons, activate by keyboard and pointer, change fragment, and revisit a fixture.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Real state activation and selector subject identity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove simulated classes, reproduce one input transition, and inspect the exact matching subject.

interactions.title: Complete value provenance
interactions.learnerAction: Complete specified and computed value rows for inherited text color, non-inherited spacing, and initial, inherit, unset, revert, and revert-layer declarations.
interactions.artifactChange: School value inspector gains missing property classification and upstream source rows.

evidence.observable: Each property and keyword reaches the defined parent, initial, origin, or layer value before computation.
evidence.changedCase: Change parent color, add a UA default, insert an earlier layer, and swap color with padding.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First incorrect defaulting branch or upstream value source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify the property, apply the keyword definition, then recompute downstream values only.

interactions.title: Finish attendance winner trace
interactions.learnerAction: Complete missing relevance, origin, importance, context, layer, specificity, scope, and order rungs for an attendance-alert property conflict.
interactions.artifactChange: Attendance alert receives one complete winner and loser provenance record.

evidence.observable: Every relevant candidate survives or loses at its first valid comparison and exactly one declaration wins.
evidence.changedCase: Toggle a condition, add user-important input, move a rule to inline context, and reorder tied candidates.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First skipped, inverted, or unsupported cascade dimension.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore candidates from the last correct rung and apply one next comparison without jumping ahead.

interactions.title: Repair functional specificity
interactions.learnerAction: Refactor school selectors using :is(), :not(), :has(), and :where() while preserving exact matches and documenting each function's A-B-C contribution.
interactions.artifactChange: Functional selectors replace escalating chains and gain column traces.

evidence.observable: Each function follows its exact argument rule, match sets remain bounded, and no decimal or carry arithmetic appears.
evidence.changedCase: Change matching branches, insert an ID argument, move it inside :where(), and mutate descendant structure.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Functional argument selection, match preservation, or column comparison.
interactions.correctionRoute: Prove match sets first, compute each argument separately, then replace the function's contribution.

interactions.title: Complete district layer architecture
interactions.learnerAction: Declare and populate district, base, components, utilities, and exceptions layers while accounting for unlayered rules and important reversal.
interactions.artifactChange: School stylesheet gains explicit layer order and repaired declaration ownership.

evidence.observable: Normal and important conflicts follow stable declared architecture without editing district source or escalating selectors.
evidence.changedCase: Increase district selector weight, reorder blocks, add an unlayered rule, and toggle importance.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Layer responsibility, first declaration order, implicit placement, or importance direction.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild layer order and responsibilities, then compare specificity only inside the surviving layer.

interactions.title: Build resilient schedule links
interactions.learnerAction: Implement schedule link, visited, hover, focus-visible, and active treatments with redundant cues, privacy bounds, and no magic-order assumption.
interactions.artifactChange: Schedule navigation gains complete state styling and an evidence matrix.

evidence.observable: All states retain link identity, keyboard focus, pointer usability, and privacy-safe output under the full cascade.
evidence.changedCase: Reorder rules, combine focus and active, remove hover capability, and visit a partitioned fixture.

interactions.feedbackTarget: State-specific winner, redundant cue, input parity, or privacy boundary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reproduce one state and input path, trace its winners, restore missing cue, then rerun all states.

interactions.title: Test user-origin changes
interactions.learnerAction: Inject normal and important user readability styles into the school portal and verify complete origin traces before and after author-layer changes.
interactions.artifactChange: Regression suite gains normal and important user-origin fixtures.

evidence.observable: User-origin candidates occupy correct priority and user-important preferences survive author specificity and layers.
evidence.changedCase: Add author-important, inline-important, later layers, and an active transition separately.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Origin and importance order across architecture mutations.
interactions.correctionRoute: Group by importance and origin before consulting author layers, specificity, or order.

interactions.title: Lower context weight
interactions.learnerAction: Replace a school-section context chain with :where() while proving unchanged target sets and lower specificity across added classes and IDs.
interactions.artifactChange: One school component context becomes zero-weight and gains before-after traces.

evidence.observable: Context arguments continue matching but contribute zero, leaving component ownership easy to override in its intended layer.
evidence.changedCase: Add an ID inside :where(), add a class outside, and move the component into another section.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Match equality and exact specificity reduction after refactor.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare complete subject sets, then count only components outside :where().

interactions.title: Defend or refuse scope
interactions.learnerAction: Explain whether repeated school cards justify @scope, name supported environments and evidence limits, or retain a simpler class architecture when scope adds no useful boundary.
interactions.artifactChange: Architecture note records a bounded scope decision and validation matrix.

evidence.observable: Decision cites real roots, limits, proximity needs, environment evidence, and a simpler alternative rather than novelty.
evidence.changedCase: Nest cards, introduce a footer limit, add a proximity tie, and remove @scope support in one named environment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Scope necessity, support claim, boundary model, and alternative quality.
interactions.correctionRoute: Draw task boundaries and conflict evidence, then choose the simplest architecture that passes named environments.

interactions.title: Test forced-color link states
interactions.learnerAction: Run schedule links through forced-color light and dark palettes and verify focus, visited orientation, hover, and active cues after author colors, shadows, and backgrounds are adjusted.
interactions.artifactChange: Link matrix gains deterministic forced-color rows and repaired redundant cues.

evidence.observable: Adjusted system colors preserve link identity and visible focus without unjustified forced-color opt-out.
evidence.changedCase: Switch light and dark palettes, suppress background images and shadows, and alter active declaration order.

interactions.feedbackTarget: State distinguishability after user-agent color adjustment.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use system-adjusted evidence, restore structural or outline cues, and retest without disabling user preferences.

interactions.title: Keep focused links visible
interactions.learnerAction: Test schedule-link focus beneath a sticky school alert at zoomed and narrow layouts, then repair geometry without advancing focus or hiding content.
interactions.artifactChange: School layout gains a focus-obstruction regression and bounded geometry repair.

evidence.observable: Keyboard-focused link remains at least partially visible and its indicator remains distinguishable at supported tablet and desktop conditions.
evidence.changedCase: Increase zoom, enlarge alert text, change viewport height, and focus links near both scroll boundaries.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Focused component visibility, indicator visibility, and cause-level geometry.
interactions.correctionRoute: Freeze focus, inspect obstruction rectangles and winning position styles, then repair the responsible layout rule.

interactions.title: Explain school cascade architecture
interactions.learnerAction: Explain how source loading, exact matches, genuine states, value provenance, origins, specificity, layers, bounded scope, and link evidence combine in the completed portal.
interactions.artifactChange: School portal stores a concise architecture and evidence defense beside its passing matrix.

evidence.observable: Defense names first decisive mechanisms, evidence limits, accessibility constraints, and remaining work without relying on selector recipes.
evidence.changedCase: Change district source weight, audience data, parent values, layer order, scope support, and link environment one at a time.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First architectural claim missing direct changed-case evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove unsupported claim, rerun its named mutation, and rewrite defense from observed provenance.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A broken civic-permit interface becomes a causal debugging record whose repairs remove invalid candidates, false provenance, inheritance errors, specificity escalation, layer inversion, scope leakage, and inaccessible link states.
activityDesigns.startingState: Clinic contains plausible screenshots and intentionally misleading comments, but the authoritative evidence surfaces expose several independent causes across source, match, cascade, and environment.
activityDesigns.completionState: Every defect has a first-divergence diagnosis, minimal source repair, changed-case regression, and explanation that distinguishes symptom from cascade cause.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback reports the observed mismatch and earliest disproven hypothesis, never names the repair before learner inspects the decisive source, match, value, or cascade evidence.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request hints narrow the failing evidence surface, then the cascade dimension, then one source region while withholding replacement declarations and final winners.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Incorrect diagnosis routes to a two-candidate parallel defect with different syntax and values, then returns to the original clinic after learner predicts the changed result.

interactions.title: Predict phantom candidate cause
interactions.learnerAction: Predict whether a reported winning rule can enter the cascade when its external source fails, its declaration is malformed, and a similar internal rule still loads.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic hypothesis board separates source failure, parser rejection, and similar-rule evidence.

evidence.observable: Alleged candidate is rejected at the earliest source or syntax boundary and cannot appear in later cascade comparisons.
evidence.changedCase: Repair only the URL, then only the declaration, and finally change the internal near-match selector.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First source-to-candidate boundary disproving the phantom winner.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace response, applicability, parsing, property, and match in order before opening the cascade ladder.

interactions.title: Inspect overbroad permit matches
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect a substring audience selector that styles residents and nonresident contractors, then identify the match-contract error before changing priority.
interactions.artifactChange: Defect record gains actual matched set, intended set, and first semantic divergence.

evidence.observable: Diagnosis proves overmatching at selector semantics before any cascade conflict and names accepted and rejected values.
evidence.changedCase: Add resident, nonresident, resident-temporary, multi-token, and uppercase audience values.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute operator and data contract rather than selector strength.
interactions.correctionRoute: List literal values, select the operator matching the contract, and rerun complete match sets.

interactions.title: Remove impossible state styling
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose a focus-visible rule attached to a nonfocusable decorative span and restore native link targeting rather than forcing a permanent focus class.
interactions.artifactChange: State selector targets the native permit link and removes simulated focus state.

evidence.observable: Keyboard navigation produces a real focus-visible match on the operable link and no decorative node pretends to own state.
evidence.changedCase: Tab forward and backward, click by pointer, move the decorative span, and remove its class.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Underlying state availability and correct interactive subject.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify the operable native element, activate genuine keyboard focus, then bind presentation to its state.

interactions.title: Repair destructive defaulting
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose an all-initial component reset that erases useful user-agent behavior and inheritance, then replace it with property-specific bounded defaulting.
interactions.artifactChange: Destructive reset becomes explicit property repairs with value provenance.

evidence.observable: Native behavior and required inheritance return while only the unwanted author values are rolled back.
evidence.changedCase: Add a button, inherited language-aware text, custom property, direction metadata, and user style.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Exact defaulting keyword boundary and collateral value loss.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace each broken property to its intended source, remove blanket reset, and restore only required rollbacks.

interactions.title: Disprove last-rule diagnosis
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why moving an author-normal rule last cannot defeat a user-important permit preference, and record the actual first decisive cascade dimension.
interactions.artifactChange: Incorrect source-order diagnosis becomes a complete origin and importance trace.

evidence.observable: User-important candidate wins before source order is consulted and author source movement changes no result.
evidence.changedCase: Move author rule first and last, add an ID, toggle importance, and remove the user declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Earliest decisive cascade dimension versus irrelevant later order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reset all candidates, sort importance and origin first, and stop once only one group survives.

interactions.title: Remove decimal specificity math
interactions.learnerAction: Replace a decimal specificity explanation that claims eleven type selectors beat one class, then repair the selector without duplicating classes or adding IDs.
interactions.artifactChange: Specificity note uses A-B-C columns and source repair lowers conflict weight.

evidence.observable: Columns compare lexicographically, no count carries, and the repaired selector remains bounded across changed markup.
evidence.changedCase: Add more type selectors, replace one class with an attribute, add :where(), and insert a new ancestor.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First invalid specificity calculation or escalation tactic.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify every selector component, compare columns left to right, then repair architecture at the decisive dimension.

interactions.title: Repair layer-direction inversion
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose an important permit declaration whose layer priority was predicted using normal direction, then repair layer ownership without moving blocks randomly.
interactions.artifactChange: Layer map gains correct normal and important directions and repaired ownership.

evidence.observable: Important declarations follow reversed author layer order while normal declarations retain forward order.
evidence.changedCase: Toggle importance, add an unlayered rule, reorder blocks without changing declarations, and add user-important input.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Importance group and layer direction before selector comparison.
interactions.correctionRoute: Select origin and importance group, choose its layer direction, then compare candidates within the winning layer.

interactions.title: Inspect leaking scope boundary
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect a nested permit widget whose scoped rule leaks past an intended footer limit, then distinguish subject inclusion from scope proximity and specificity.
interactions.artifactChange: Scope map records roots, limits, included subjects, and first boundary error.

evidence.observable: Only subjects inside the defined root and before the limit enter scoped comparisons; proximity applies after inclusion and specificity.
evidence.changedCase: Move the footer, nest a second widget, add an equal-specificity rule, and run an environment without @scope.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Scope inclusion, limit, proximity, or support claim at first divergence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name environment, draw root and limit, filter subjects, then compare eligible declarations.

interactions.title: Restore overridden focus
interactions.learnerAction: Test a permit link whose active and utility rules remove its focus-visible outline, then repair the first decisive cascade conflict and preserve combined states.
interactions.artifactChange: Focus rule and layer ownership are repaired with combined-state regression rows.

evidence.observable: Keyboard focus remains visible during idle and active states and survives utility and layer mutations.
evidence.changedCase: Press and hold activation, reorder utilities, toggle importance, enable forced colors, and add a sticky obstruction.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First cascade rule that removes focus paint or visibility.
interactions.correctionRoute: Freeze focus plus active state, trace winning paint and geometry, and repair the earliest responsible dimension.

interactions.title: Explain causal clinic repairs
interactions.learnerAction: Explain each clinic repair from first divergence through changed-case evidence and defend why no stronger selector, blanket reset, vendor edit, forced state, or privacy leak remains.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic stores a complete causal repair defense and regression matrix.

evidence.observable: Every repair has a disproven alternative, minimal source change, complete trace, and passing changed environment or state.
evidence.changedCase: Reintroduce each original symptom through a different source, selector, layer, scope, or environment mutation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Unsupported causal claim, overbroad repair, or missing regression evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove one unproven explanation, rerun its mutation, and rebuild defense from first-divergence evidence.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: Learner independently architects a regional newsroom stylesheet for wire-service content, local components, reader preferences, live states, corrections, and evidence-backed link behavior.
activityDesigns.startingState: Newsroom provides semantic article and navigation HTML, stakeholder constraints, independent acceptance tests, and untouched external wire styles but no stylesheet recipe or copied portal architecture.
activityDesigns.completionState: Newsroom passes exact source, match, value, cascade, specificity, layer, bounded-scope, state, preference, privacy, and responsive accessibility evidence with a professional defense.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback identifies the unmet acceptance invariant and observed evidence only; learner must choose architecture, selectors, values, tests, and repairs without workshop ordering.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request hints name a competency or evidence surface, never the rule, selector, layer order, scope boundary, winning declaration, or final state styling.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Failure creates a parallel changed newsroom fixture and requires a new causal test before the original artifact can be reassessed under another mutation set.

interactions.title: Build newsroom source inventory
interactions.learnerAction: Inventory every local, wire-service, print, reader, internal, and inline style source and collect valid declaration candidates for selected newsroom properties.
interactions.artifactChange: Newsroom gains a source provenance and candidate-declaration ledger.

evidence.observable: Every candidate traces to a valid applicable source, exact property, match set, and document-order position.
evidence.changedCase: Change a source URL, media condition, parse boundary, internal block position, and wire filename independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source-to-candidate provenance completeness and first unsupported row.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace the disputed row through network, applicability, parsing, property, match, and order before editing.

interactions.title: Build exact content and state hooks
interactions.learnerAction: Choose bounded attribute hooks and genuine pseudo-class states for breaking, corrected, subscriber, section, target, pointer, keyboard, and activation requirements.
interactions.artifactChange: Newsroom stylesheet gains proportional hooks without invented semantic state or permanent interaction classes.

evidence.observable: Complete subject sets follow content contracts and real browser states across every named requirement.
evidence.changedCase: Mutate status and section data, nest an icon, move focus, remove hover capability, activate a link, and change target.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Exact subject set and genuine state behind each styling requirement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate persistent data from transient state, prove each match set, then revise only the overbroad hook.

interactions.title: Build newsroom value policy
interactions.learnerAction: Choose inherited and non-inherited property boundaries plus any initial, inherit, unset, revert, revert-layer, or bounded all usage from explicit newsroom needs.
interactions.artifactChange: Newsroom gains deliberate defaulting declarations and property value-provenance notes.

evidence.observable: Each defaulting keyword selects the intended source without erasing native behavior, reader preferences, semantics, or unrelated properties.
evidence.changedCase: Change parent text values, add reader styles, add wire defaults, remove local declarations, and inspect print output.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Property classification and keyword-selected upstream source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name intended value source per property, trace current provenance, and replace only mismatched defaulting choices.

interactions.title: Test reader style origins
interactions.learnerAction: Test normal and important reader styles against newsroom normal, important, inline, animated, transitioned, layered, and later-source declarations.
interactions.artifactChange: Acceptance matrix gains reader-origin conflict traces for selected properties.

evidence.observable: Complete origin and importance order preserves reader-important choices and accurately places time-bound values.
evidence.changedCase: Toggle each importance, start and stop transition, add inline style, and reorder local sources.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First origin, importance, context, or timeline classification error.
interactions.correctionRoute: Freeze one state and time, collect candidates, sort origin and importance, then resume later dimensions.

interactions.title: Build low-conflict selectors
interactions.learnerAction: Author newsroom selectors with deliberate A-B-C columns and bounded :is(), :not(), :has(), and :where() use while refusing duplicated-class or ID escalation.
interactions.artifactChange: Newsroom gains maintainable selectors plus match and specificity evidence.

evidence.observable: Selectors target exact subjects, follow every functional rule, and remain predictable after new sections and state hooks appear.
evidence.changedCase: Add a new ancestor, ID argument, state class, corrected article, and repeated component in another section.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Match scope, functional contribution, or unjustified selector weight.
interactions.correctionRoute: Prove intended subjects, compute A-B-C, and remove weight not required by either matching or architecture.

interactions.title: Architect newsroom layers
interactions.learnerAction: Define layer order and ownership for wire, base, components, utilities, reader-safe exceptions, and unlayered policy without editing wire-service source.
interactions.artifactChange: Newsroom stylesheet gains explicit layer architecture and external-source integration.

evidence.observable: Normal and important winners follow declared responsibilities, external source stays unchanged, and unlayered rules follow written policy.
evidence.changedCase: Increase wire selector weight, reorder its source, add a new component, add unlayered normal input, and toggle importance.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Layer ownership, first declaration order, unlayered policy, or important reversal.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore source ownership, redraw layer map, then relocate only declarations whose responsibility is wrong.

interactions.title: Defend bounded scope choice
interactions.learnerAction: Defend use or refusal of @scope for repeated story cards using roots, limits, proximity need, named environment support, work-in-progress status, and simpler alternatives.
interactions.artifactChange: Architecture record gains evidence-backed scope decision and environment matrix.

evidence.observable: Decision is bounded to actual conflict and environments, with complete subject inclusion and proximity evidence where scope is used.
evidence.changedCase: Nest cards, add a footer limit, create equal-specificity proximity conflict, and disable scope in one environment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Necessity, boundary, proximity, maturity, support, or alternative claim.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove novelty claims, trace actual component conflict, and select the simplest architecture passing named cases.

interactions.title: Build newsroom link states
interactions.learnerAction: Design headline and navigation link, visited, hover, focus-visible, and active states with full cascade reasoning, redundant cues, and privacy-safe boundaries.
interactions.artifactChange: Newsroom gains complete state styling and a link behavior matrix.

evidence.observable: Every link state remains identifiable and operable without exposing history or assuming declaration order alone.
evidence.changedCase: Reorder rules, combine states, change selector weight, add reader-important focus, and visit partitioned fixtures.

interactions.feedbackTarget: State-specific winner, non-color cue, focus, input parity, or privacy limit.
interactions.correctionRoute: Activate one real state, trace all relevant properties, restore the missing invariant, then rerun every state.

interactions.title: Test forced-color newsroom
interactions.learnerAction: Test all newsroom link and focus states in forced-color light and dark palettes after author colors, shadows, and backgrounds are system-adjusted.
interactions.artifactChange: Link matrix gains deterministic forced-color evidence and any needed redundant-cue repairs.

evidence.observable: System-adjusted output preserves link identity and visible focus without unnecessary forced-color opt-out.
evidence.changedCase: Switch both palettes, suppress shadows and background images, alter active order, and inject reader-important colors.

interactions.feedbackTarget: State and focus distinguishability under system color replacement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect adjusted used output, restore outline or structural cues, and retest while preserving user adjustment.

interactions.title: Test newsroom focus visibility
