interactions.learnerAction: Navigate every link by keyboard at supported tablet and desktop layouts, zoomed text, long headlines, and breaking-news overlays while recording focus visibility and obstruction.
interactions.artifactChange: Newsroom regression matrix gains focus indicator and obstruction geometry evidence.

evidence.observable: Each focused component remains visibly indicated and at least partly unobscured without advancing focus or hiding news content.
evidence.changedCase: Increase text size, lengthen headlines, resize overlay, change viewport height, and focus near both scroll edges.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Focus paint winner and author-created obstruction geometry.
interactions.correctionRoute: Freeze failing focus, inspect paint and rectangles, repair first responsible rule, then retest whole path.

interactions.title: Run cascade mutation suite
interactions.learnerAction: Mutate relevance, origin, importance, style context, layer, specificity, scope proximity, and order independently and require a fresh complete winner trace each time.
interactions.artifactChange: Newsroom stores a dimension-isolated cascade mutation suite.

evidence.observable: Each mutation changes only expected candidate or comparison dimensions and yields one evidence-backed winner.
evidence.changedCase: Execute one isolated mutation for every named cascade dimension and then one combined adversarial case.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First mutation with unexplained candidate or winner change.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore baseline, apply one mutation, compare ladder rows, and repair the earliest unexpected divergence.

interactions.title: Run value provenance mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Mutate parent values, declaration absence, property inheritance class, keyword, user-agent source, author layer, and display environment across newsroom components.
interactions.artifactChange: Value inspector stores changed declared-through-actual provenance traces.

evidence.observable: Every specified and computed result points to the correct defaulting or cascade source and updates only for relevant mutations.
evidence.changedCase: Run inherited, non-inherited, initial, inherit, unset, revert, revert-layer, and forced-environment mutations.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First value-processing stage whose expected dependency change is wrong.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare baseline and mutation at each stage, stop at first difference, and repair its source or model.

interactions.title: Run selector weight mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Change matching branches and A-B-C components across ordinary and functional selectors while asserting exact subject sets and conflict outcomes.
interactions.artifactChange: Selector suite records complete match and column traces under adversarial changes.

evidence.observable: No decimal arithmetic, hidden branch weight, or unnecessary escalation survives changed structure and state.
evidence.changedCase: Insert ID, class, type, :is(), :not(), :has(), :where(), and child-index argument variations.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First mismatch between subject set, functional rule, column total, and winner.
interactions.correctionRoute: Prove subjects, categorize components, apply function rule, then compare columns and cascade.

interactions.title: Defend cascade architecture
interactions.learnerAction: Defend source ownership, defaulting policy, conflict order, selector weight, layer responsibilities, scope limits, and rejected alternatives using changed-case evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Newsroom gains a professional architecture decision record with evidence limits.

evidence.observable: Defense explains why architecture remains predictable under source, state, reader, vendor, and environment changes.
evidence.changedCase: Update wire source, insert a reader preference, change layer order, remove scope support, and add a new component.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First decision lacking a traced alternative, tradeoff, or changed-case result.
interactions.correctionRoute: State the decision question, compare bounded alternatives under one mutation, and revise only evidence-backed claims.

interactions.title: Defend reader link behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Defend newsroom link and focus behavior to editorial and accessibility stakeholders using keyboard, pointer, forced-color, privacy, obstruction, and cascade evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Final newsroom evidence package gains stakeholder-facing link behavior defense and unresolved risks.

evidence.observable: Defense separates verified behavior from environment limits and identifies responsible next work without overstating conformance.
evidence.changedCase: Review new long headlines, changed overlay, alternate reader palette, no-hover device, partitioned history, and reordered state rules.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First accessibility, privacy, cascade, or environment claim exceeding evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Bound the claim to tested matrix cells, record unresolved environment, and propose a specific next verification.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A delayed cascade casebook reconstructs candidate matching, source loading, value defaulting, precedence, specificity, layers, scope, and link-state behavior from unfamiliar evidence.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives new stylesheets, DOM fragments, computed-value records, scope trees, interaction histories, and reader settings without access to prior activity answers.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner retrieves all nine concepts, corrects each missed causal link on a parallel case, and schedules another delayed retrieval attempt.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names the first missing decision stage only after commitment and never exposes the final winning declaration before correction.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request hints cue the evidence category, contrast two candidates, then provide a smaller parallel trace.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Every miss requires a causal explanation and a different changed-case result before the review record is accepted.

interactions.title: Retrieve declaration candidates
interactions.learnerAction: Reconstruct selector, declaration, property, value, importance, and source-order boundaries for three unfamiliar candidate rules.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains a parsed declaration-candidate ledger.

evidence.observable: Every candidate points to the exact selector and declaration capable of entering the named-property cascade.
evidence.changedCase: Add a second declaration, an invalid value, and a nonmatching selector branch.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First rule boundary or candidate-status error.
interactions.correctionRoute: Parse one rule boundary at a time, name the target property, then re-evaluate the changed source.

interactions.title: Retrieve loaded source evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Identify which linked, embedded, and inline author sources actually load and contribute declarations before ranking any candidate.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains a loaded-source provenance record.

evidence.observable: Included and excluded sources are justified by document and network evidence rather than assumed file names.
evidence.changedCase: Change media applicability, break one URL, and move one declaration into a style attribute.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First unloaded or inapplicable source treated as a cascade candidate.
interactions.correctionRoute: Recheck document linkage and resource result before tracing declarations.

interactions.title: Retrieve attribute matching
interactions.learnerAction: Recompute presence, token, prefix, and case-modified attribute matches that decide whether candidate declarations remain relevant.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains an attribute match-set checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Each candidate is retained or discarded using the exact attribute value and operator contract.
evidence.changedCase: Replace a token list, alter case, remove an attribute, and append a path segment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First loose-substring or case assumption changing relevance.
interactions.correctionRoute: Write the operator contract and compare the literal changed value again.

interactions.title: Retrieve active state candidates
interactions.learnerAction: Reconstruct which focus, hover, active, link, visited, target, and form states are true at each point in an unfamiliar interaction timeline.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains a time-indexed state candidate record.

evidence.observable: State candidates follow actual browser and document conditions rather than selector declaration order.
evidence.changedCase: Switch input modality, activate the link, change the URL fragment, and invalidate a control.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First pseudo-class treated as a state creator or static source attribute.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name the state owner and triggering event, then replay the timeline.

interactions.title: Predict inherited and initial values
interactions.learnerAction: Predict specified and computed results for inherited and non-inherited properties across a changed parent-child tree.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains a parent-to-child value provenance trace.

evidence.observable: Each result distinguishes winning declaration, inherited value, and initial value at the correct value stage.
evidence.changedCase: Move the child, remove its declaration, change the parent value, and substitute a non-inherited property.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First claim that every property inherits or absent declarations mean no value.
interactions.correctionRoute: Check the property inheritance definition and trace the computed parent value.

interactions.title: Explain defaulting keyword differences
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why initial, inherit, unset, revert, and revert-layer produce different results in a layered component case.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains a keyword-to-cascade-stage explanation.

evidence.observable: Explanation traces the correct origin, layer, inheritance behavior, and value source without calling the browser default universal.
evidence.changedCase: Switch property inheritance, move the rule between layers, and introduce a reader-origin declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First keyword collapsed into a generic reset meaning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate one keyword against property inheritance and the preceding cascade level.

interactions.title: Predict origin and importance precedence
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the winning declaration among user-agent, user, author, animation, transition, and important candidates before considering specificity.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains an origin-and-importance precedence rung.

evidence.observable: Winner and discarded candidates follow cascade origin and importance order including user preference authority.
evidence.changedCase: Toggle importance, add a user rule, start an animation, and remove a transition.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First assumption that author specificity always outranks user or dynamic origins.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rank origin and importance before writing any specificity columns.

interactions.title: Correct a last-rule-wins trace
interactions.learnerAction: Correct a trace that jumped to source order and reconstruct relevance, origin, context, layer, specificity, scope proximity, and order.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains a complete corrected winner trace.

evidence.observable: Every comparison stage is applied only to candidates still tied after the previous stage.
evidence.changedCase: Reorder sources, change one state, move a rule into a layer, and wrap another in a nearer scope.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First skipped or misordered cascade decision stage.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to the last verified tied candidate set and apply exactly one next stage.

interactions.title: Retrieve specificity columns
interactions.learnerAction: Reconstruct A-B-C specificity columns for type, pseudo-element, class, attribute, pseudo-class, ID, and style-attribute candidates.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains an auditable specificity ledger.

evidence.observable: Each selector contribution appears in the correct lexicographic column without decimal conversion or combinator weight.
evidence.changedCase: Add a pseudo-element, attribute, combinator, ID, and inline declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First specificity token counted in the wrong column.
interactions.correctionRoute: Mark selector tokens, ignore combinators, then total A, B, and C separately.

interactions.title: Predict functional pseudo-class weight
interactions.learnerAction: Predict specificity for :is(), :not(), :has(), :where(), and child-index selectors after their argument lists mutate.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains functional-selector specificity comparisons.

evidence.observable: The most specific relevant argument replaces functional pseudo-class weight, :where() remains zero, and child indexing adds its own pseudo-class contribution.
evidence.changedCase: Insert an ID argument, wrap a branch in :where(), and alter an of-selector list.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First functional pseudo-class assigned a fixed uniform weight.
interactions.correctionRoute: Apply the named function rule before comparing A-B-C columns.

interactions.title: Correct inverted layer precedence
interactions.learnerAction: Correct a layer trace that ranks normal and important declarations identically and explain the declared layer order.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains corrected normal and important layer maps.

evidence.observable: Normal and important layer comparisons follow their distinct ordering while unlayered and reordered candidates are explicit.
evidence.changedCase: Reverse layer declarations, add an unlayered rule, and toggle importance.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First assumption that importance changes only value strength, not layer ordering.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate normal from important candidates and rebuild each ordered layer list.

interactions.title: Explain scope boundaries and proximity
interactions.learnerAction: Explain candidate eligibility, lower boundary exclusion, scope proximity, and the draft-status implementation bound for an unfamiliar component tree.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains a bounded scope decision explanation.

evidence.observable: Explanation separates scoping eligibility from specificity and applies proximity only after specificity among remaining tied scoped declarations.
evidence.changedCase: Nest a second scope root, add a lower boundary, change selector weight, and evaluate an unsupported environment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First claim that scope roots add specificity or draft support is universal.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace DOM hops and candidate ties while recording the implementation assumption explicitly.

interactions.title: Predict accessible link states
interactions.learnerAction: Predict link, visited, hover, focus-visible, and active results across keyboard, pointer, forced-color, and history-privacy conditions.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains a cross-environment link behavior matrix.

evidence.observable: Each result uses actual simultaneous matches, full cascade evidence, a non-color cue, visible focus, system colors, and bounded visited claims.
evidence.changedCase: Reorder state rules, remove hover, enable forced colors, obscure focus, and change history state.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First mnemonic-order, color-only, focus-removal, or visited-data assumption.
interactions.correctionRoute: List simultaneous states and environment constraints before tracing each property.

interactions.title: Correct an integrated cascade account
interactions.learnerAction: Correct a peer account of one changed component by citing exact source, match, state, value, origin, layer, specificity, scope, order, and accessibility evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook closes with a corrected end-to-end causal trace and rescheduled weak concepts.

evidence.observable: Correction identifies the first false premise, repairs the complete result, and records which concept needs later retrieval.
evidence.changedCase: Change source availability, DOM state, reader rule, layer statement, selector function, scope root, and focus environment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First unsupported conclusion or omitted dependency in the integrated account.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to the earliest incorrect evidence stage, recompute downstream results, and schedule the missed concept.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An independent cascade checkpoint produces auditable answers, code, debugging traces, and architecture defenses across novel document, component, reader-preference, and interaction cases.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives unfamiliar requirements, documents, stylesheets, browser evidence, mutation cases, and no workshop implementation or assessment hints.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner independently proves all nine concepts through changed cases; canonical grading remains server-side and every failed invariant requires a different reassessment case.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Assessment returns passed and failed evidence categories only after submission; it never exposes canonical declarations, rankings, or defenses.
activityDesigns.hintContract: No hints, answer reveal, or workshop artifact access is available during the assessment attempt.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A failed category routes to named prerequisite retrieval and a novel reassessment variant rather than answer imitation.

interactions.title: Classify candidate declarations
interactions.learnerAction: Answer which exact declarations can compete for two named properties and justify every rejected source fragment.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint records an independently classified candidate set.

evidence.observable: Answer preserves rule boundaries, property identity, validity, and declaration importance before ranking.
evidence.changedCase: Add a malformed selector branch, repeated property, shorthand, and unrelated declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Candidate classification invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to rule anatomy retrieval, then classify a new property set.

interactions.title: Debug missing style provenance
interactions.learnerAction: Debug a computed result by isolating document linkage, fetch, media applicability, parse, and inline-source evidence before editing CSS.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint records the first broken source stage and a verified repair.

evidence.observable: Repair changes the intended loaded-source evidence and preserves unrelated source ordering.
evidence.changedCase: Rename the asset, change media, inject a parse error, and move one value inline.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source provenance and minimal-repair invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to application/loading diagnosis, then inspect a new source graph.

interactions.title: Code a bounded attribute hook
interactions.learnerAction: Code the narrowest attribute selectors for status, language, and resource-type requirements, then prove accepted and rejected match sets.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint implementation gains bounded attribute hooks and match fixtures.

evidence.observable: Only intended elements enter the cascade across token, prefix, case, missing, and empty value cases.
evidence.changedCase: Vary tokens, language subtags, URL suffixes, value case, and absent attributes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute hook precision invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to attribute-operator retrieval, then implement a different attribute contract.

interactions.title: Debug a false state assumption
interactions.learnerAction: Debug a component whose selector matches the wrong focus, form, target, or interaction state by replaying browser-owned transitions.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint records a repaired state contract and transition evidence.

evidence.observable: Repair follows actual state ownership and works across keyboard, pointer, URL, and form mutations.
evidence.changedCase: Switch focus modality, alter validity timing, change fragment, and remove pointer hover.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pseudo-class state-source invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to state-source retrieval, then diagnose a different event sequence.

interactions.title: Trace CSS value stages
interactions.learnerAction: Answer how declared, cascaded, specified, computed, used, and actual values differ for a property in a changed layout environment.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint records a stage-by-stage value provenance answer.

evidence.observable: Each stage is tied to observable inputs and does not confuse cascade winner with final rendered measurement.
evidence.changedCase: Change parent font, viewport geometry, available font, declaration validity, and device rounding.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS value-stage invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to value provenance modeling, then trace a different property.

interactions.title: Code intentional defaulting
interactions.learnerAction: Code initial, inherit, unset, revert, or revert-layer only where the requirement demands its exact semantics and prove each choice under mutation.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint implementation gains explicit, evidence-backed defaulting behavior.

evidence.observable: Each keyword result matches property inheritance and the intended origin or layer boundary without erasing reader preferences.
evidence.changedCase: Switch inherited property, reorder layers, add user styling, and remove a component declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Defaulting keyword semantic invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to keyword contrasts, then code a new property-and-layer case.

interactions.title: Rank origin and importance
interactions.learnerAction: Answer the winner and full discarded-candidate order across user-agent, user, author, animation, transition, normal, and important declarations.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint records an independent precedence ladder.

evidence.observable: Ranking preserves reader-origin authority and dynamic-value precedence without using specificity prematurely.
evidence.changedCase: Toggle importance, terminate transition, pause animation, and add a user declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Cascade origin and importance invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to precedence modeling, then rank a new candidate set.

interactions.title: Debug a complete cascade conflict
interactions.learnerAction: Debug the first false decision in a computed-style trace spanning relevance, origin, context, layer, specificity, scope proximity, and order.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint records a minimal fix and complete before-and-after causal trace.

evidence.observable: Diagnosis repairs the earliest incorrect stage and recomputes all downstream tied candidates.
evidence.changedCase: Change matching state, reader origin, layer membership, selector specificity, scope root, and source order.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Complete cascade-order invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to the failed decision stage, then debug a new conflict graph.

interactions.title: Compare specificity columns
interactions.learnerAction: Answer A-B-C specificity for unfamiliar selector pairs and identify when specificity is irrelevant because an earlier cascade stage decides.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint records column traces and bounded comparisons.

evidence.observable: Columns are lexicographic, tokens are classified correctly, and no score is compared across different origins or layers.
evidence.changedCase: Add IDs, attributes, pseudo-elements, combinators, importance, and separate layers.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Specificity calculation and applicability invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to column tracing, then compare new selectors after earlier stages tie.

interactions.title: Code low-friction functional selectors
interactions.learnerAction: Code :is(), :not(), :has(), :where(), or child-index selectors that meet behavior requirements while preserving a stated specificity budget.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint implementation gains tested selectors and a specificity budget ledger.

evidence.observable: Selectors match intended changed trees and remain within the declared A-B-C ceiling after argument-list mutations.
evidence.changedCase: Insert a high-specificity argument, extra sibling, nested child, and :where() wrapper.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Functional-selector behavior and specificity-budget invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to functional specificity rules, then code against a new DOM fixture.

interactions.title: Debug a layer contract regression
interactions.learnerAction: Debug a regression caused by layer declaration order, nested names, imports, unlayered rules, or reversed important precedence without raising selector weight.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint records a repaired layer contract and mutation suite.

evidence.observable: Minimal repair restores intended normal and important precedence while preserving reader-origin and third-party boundaries.
evidence.changedCase: Reorder layer statements, add an import, toggle importance, and introduce an unlayered override.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Cascade layer architecture invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to separate normal and important layer maps, then debug a new layer graph.

interactions.title: Defend a bounded scope decision
interactions.learnerAction: Defend whether to use @scope for a component, including root, limit, proximity, specificity, support evidence, and a simpler verified architecture when support is absent.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint records a standards-bounded component scope decision.

evidence.observable: Defense distinguishes current draft behavior from verified implementation support and proves both selected and unsupported-environment paths.
evidence.changedCase: Nest scope roots, add a lower boundary, change proximity, and remove @scope support.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Scope semantics, support, and architectural-justification invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to scope eligibility and proximity evidence, then defend a different component boundary.

interactions.title: Code robust link interactions
interactions.learnerAction: Code distinguishable link, visited, hover, focus-visible, and active behavior from requirements, then test simultaneous states instead of applying a memorized order.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint implementation gains a tested cross-input link-state system.

evidence.observable: Links retain non-color distinction, visible focus, meaningful activation feedback, and correct property winners across actual simultaneous matches.
evidence.changedCase: Reorder declarations, combine focus and hover, remove pointer hover, change history, and activate the link.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Link state, cascade, and non-color distinction invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to simultaneous-state matrices, then implement a new navigation fixture.

interactions.title: Debug link environment failures
interactions.learnerAction: Debug focus obstruction, forced-color adjustment, reader-style conflict, and visited-history overclaim using browser evidence and privacy-bounded expectations.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint records repaired link behavior and bounded environment claims.

evidence.observable: Repair preserves visible unobscured focus and system-color meaning, respects reader declarations, and does not infer visited data beyond permitted rendering.
evidence.changedCase: Enable forced colors, add sticky overlap, inject user important styling, partition history, and disable hover.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Focus, forced-color, user-origin, and privacy invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to the failed environment matrix cell, then debug a different palette and overlay.

interactions.title: Defend the complete cascade architecture
interactions.learnerAction: Defend an unfamiliar interface architecture with source, match, state, value, origin, layer, specificity, scope, link, accessibility, and changed-case evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Checkpoint closes with an auditable architecture record, limitations, and independent transfer evidence.

evidence.observable: Defense ties each decision to causal runtime evidence, respects user control and draft bounds, and survives every required mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Swap source delivery, mutate attributes and states, add reader preferences, reorder layers, alter selector arguments, remove scope support, and change input environment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Integrated correctness, transfer, accessibility, and evidence invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to the first failed concept gate, then defend a novel architecture after targeted retrieval.

authorshipBoundary.interpretation: This file fixes evidence, sequence, interaction, feedback, and correction requirements only; original learner-facing explanations, fixtures, checks, answers, and responsive workspace behavior remain unauthored and unpublished.

gaps: Independent subject, instructional-design, assessment, accessibility, safety, and duplication reviews have not accepted this step architecture.
Learner-facing copy, source fixtures, canonical server-side checks, persisted correction scheduling, browser and assistive-technology evidence, and learner pilots do not yet exist.
